steps.title: Match Morris to the pattern
steps.content: The first sentence states the narrator's general observation: in a boat, each crew member thinks he is doing everything. The underlined sentence then gives Morris's version of that belief. He thinks he alone has been working while Arthur and the narrator have been imposing on him.

steps.title: Identify the specific example
steps.content: Choice A wrongly treats the sentence as Morris's reaction to the narrator's idea. Choice C invents a focus on navigation, and Choice D invents confusion. Morris's belief is not a new topic; it is a concrete example of the narrator's broader boating trend. The answer is B.
